localeBox: Select Locale Language of the app.
threadsBox:
The maximum allowed number of parallel CPU threads for either generation or drawing.
At least one of the parallel check boxes below must be checked for this to apply.
Turn it down from the maximum if your system is already busy elsewhere, or if you want some spare CPU threads for other stuff.
The generation should run the fastest if you tune this to the exact number of free available CPU threads.
The maximum on this slider is the number of all CPU threads, but not only the free ones.
cacheBox:
Should the fractal be generated with special new algorithm that aim to be friendly with cache optimization?
It doesn't actually seem to help the performance, it seems to be slower, so I recommend to keep this off.'
setButTip: Open glboal app settings. Basic performance, language, etc.
genButTip:
Scheduling manager of Fractal Animation Generators.
Create your animations here.
Schedule a batch many generations and exports by settings up the properties, then slecting to generate animations, and scheduling exports.
And it will all be done without further input.
editButTip: Fractal Editor - craft youw own fractal rules!
helpButTip: How to use? And other information about the app.
sizeBox: Scale of children inside (how much to scale the image when switching parent<->child)
maxBox:
The root scale. If too small, the fractal might not fill the whole screen. If too large, it might hurt the performance.
If pieces of the fractal are disappearing at the CORNERS, you should increase it. If not, you can try decreasing a little.
minBox:
How tiny the iterations must be to get before rendering dots (if too large, it might crash. If too low it might look gray and have bad performance).
 Try setting the detail parameter to maximum, and if the fractal starts dithering when zooming, you should decrease the minSize.
cutBox:
A scaling multiplier to test cutting off iterations that are completely outside the view (if you see pieces disappearing too early when zooming in, increase it. If not, you can decrease to boost performance).
If you see pieces of fractals disappearing near the EDGES, you should increase it.
angleBox: Type the name for a new children angle set, if you wish to add one.
addAngleButton: Add a new children angle set.
removeAngleButton: Remove the selected children angle set from this fractal.
colorBox: Type the name for a new children color set, if you wish to add one.
addColorButton: Add a new children color set.
removeColorButton: Remove the selected children color set from this fractal.
addCut: Add a CutFunction (so far only the pre-coded are available)
loadButton: Load a fractal definition from a file.
saveButton: Save the selected fractal definition to a file.
removeCutButton: remove the selected CutFunction from this fractal.
fractalSelect: Select the type of fractal to generate
cutparamBox: Type the cutFunction seed. (-1 for random)
resX: Type the X resolution of the render (width)
resY: Type the Y resolution of the render (height)
removeResolution: Remove the selected resolution. First two are special and not allowed to be removed!
addResolution: Add the Resolution in the boxes to the list.
resSelect: Select a rendering resolution (the second choice is the custom resolution you can type in the boxes to the left
paletteSelect: Choose the color palette. Then the children colors set will determine how the colors from this palette progress deep into the iteration
removePalette:
Removes this selected palette from the list.
If it's a default one, it will be restored on the next launch.
addPalette: Add your own custom palette to the list. You will be prompted with a series of ColorDialogs, cancelling another color pick will finish the palette.
defaultHue: Type the initial hue angle of the first image (in degrees).
nameBox: Name this generator. Only the window name for bookkeeping in generator list.
generationSelect:
Only Image: Will only render one still image. Cannot export PNG animation or GIF animation, only a single image, the first one from a zoom animation.
Animation: Will render the animation as set up by your settings.
All Seeds: Will not zoom/spin/shift at all, but instead cycle through all the available CutFunction seeds.
encodePngSelect: Yes: Will export animation as PNG series, that will enable you to export the PNGS or MP4 quicker after it's finished, but not really quicker overall.
encodeGifSelect:
No: Will not encode a gif. You will not be able to export a GIF afterwards, but you can switch to a Local/Global later, and it will not fully reset the generator. It just catches up with missing frames.
Local: Will encode a GIF during the generation, so you could save it when finished. With a local colormap (one for each frame). Higher color precision. Slow encoding, larger GIF file size.
Global: Also encodes a GIF. But with a global color map learned from a first frame. Not recommended for changing colors. Much faster encoding, smaller GIF file size.
voidSelect:
What and how will render the depth of void? Just original CPU, or some of the GPU variants? 
 BFS should have identical quality to CPU, Fast Flood should be the fastest. Jump Flood in-between.
drawSelect: What and how will draw the finished image? Just original CPU, or some of the GPU variants? Functions recommended for performance.
ditherBox: Temporal Dithering: if the real color is in-between 2 byte values, it evaluates randomly to the lower or higher one, with the probability respectively.
periodBox:
How many frames for the self-similar center child to zoom in to the same size as the parent if you are zooming in.
Or for the parent to zoom out to the same size as the child if you are zooming out.
This will equal the number of generated frames of the animation if the center child is the same color.
Or it will be a third of the number of generated frames of the animation if the center child is a different color.
periodMultiplierBox: Multiplies the frame count, slowing down the rotation and hue shifts.
zoomSelect: Choose in which direction you want the fractal zoom.
defaultZoomBox: Type the initial zoom of the first image in number of skipped frames. -1 for random
hueSelect:
Choose the color scheme of the fractal.
Also, you can make the color hues slowly cycle as the fractal zooms.
hueSpeedBox:
Type the extra speed on the hue shifting from the values possible for looping.
Only possible if you have chosen color cycling on the left.
spinSelect: Choose in which direction you want the zoom animation to spin, or to not spin.
defaultAngleBox: Type the initial angle of the first image (in degrees).
spinSpeedBox: Type the extra speed on the spinning from the values possible for looping.
ambBox:
The strength of the ambient grey color in the empty spaces far away between the generated fractal dots.
-1 for transparent
noiseBox: The strength of the random noise in the empty spaces far away between the generated fractal dots.
voidBox: Scale of the void noise, so it's more visible and compressible at higher resolutions.
detailBox: Level of Detail (The lower the finer).
saturateBox:
Enhance the color saturation of the fractal dots.
Useful, when the fractal is too gray, like the Sierpinski Triangle (TriFlake).
brightnessBox: Brightness level: 0% black, 100% normalized maximum, 300% overexposed 3x maximum.
bloomBox: Bloom: 0 will be maximally crisp, but possibly dark with thick fractals. Higher values will blur/bloom out the fractal dots.
blurBox: Motion blur: Lowest is no blur and fast generation, highest 10 smeared frames 10 times slower generation.
zoomChildBox: Which child to focus and zoom into? 0 is center.
abortBox: How many milliseconds of pause after the last settings change until the generator restarts?
timingSelect: Choose if you want to set a delay or framerate. Delay will be precisely used for GIF export, and framerate from MP4 export.
timing0:
A delay between frames in 1/100 of a second for the preview and exported GIF file, so if you want to export a GIF, make sure to set this instead of framerate to avoid rounding errors.
The framerate will be roughly 100/delay
timing1: The frame rate per second (roughly 100/delay). Used for exporting Mp4, so if you want to export MP4, make sure to set this instead of delay to avoid rounding errors
parallelTypeSelect:
Select which parallelism to be used if the left checkBox is enabled.
Of Animation = Batching animation frames, recommended for Animations with perfect pixels.
Of Depth/Of Recursion = parallel single image generation, recommended for fast single images, 1 in a million pixels might be slightly wrong
stripeBox:
Cache optimization parameter: Into how large horizontal stripes would the image be subdivided and the rendering sorted?
 There should fit at least twice as many stripes into the image's height. And it should be small enough to fit into cache.
If either Bin or Stripe is left at zero, the app will try to guess some good estimation of both.
binBox:
Cache optimization parameter: How many hundreds of dots will fit into one bin on each stripe, before a next bin is initialized?(1 dot is about 18 bytes)
If either Bin or Stripe are left at zero, the app will try to guess some good estimation of both.
l2Box:
Cache optimization parameter: How large is the L2 cache of one thread of your CPU? (in kilobytes)
This is hard for the app to know. If left zero, it will only do an educated guess.
prevButton:
Stop the animation and move to the previous frame.
Useful for selecting the exact frame you want to export to a PNG file.
animateButton:
Toggle preview animation.
Will seamlessly loop when the fractal is finished generating.
Clicking on the image does the same thing.
nextButton:
Stop the animation and move to the next frame.
Useful for selecting the exact frame you want to export to a PNG file.
restartButton: Restarts the generator. Maybe useful for randomized settings, but to be safe you have to click it twice in a row.
exportButton0:
Export the currently displayed frame into a PNG file.
Stop the animation and select the frame you wish to export with the buttons above.
exportButton1: Export the full animation into a series of PNG files
exportButton2: Export the full animation into MP4 video
exportButton3:
Export the full animation into a GIF file.
Must have GIF encoding enabled.
exportButton4:
Convert a GIF file into Mp4.
Must have GIF encoding enabled.
exportButton5:
This will import a fractal instead.
If you select any exported fractal, it will attempt to load everything it can to replicate it.
The export must have the original suggested export filename.
exportButton6:
This will import a fractal instead.
If you have an Import Code, you can paste it, and it will preselect all the fractal definitions and settings.
exportSelect:
Select what you want to save with the button on the left.
Hover over that button after the selection to get more info about the selection.
fileSelect: Toggle what selected properties should be used for a default export file name.
debugBox: Shows a log of task and image states to see what the generator is doing.
exportStillSaving:
Your export is still saving!
Are you sure you want to close the application and potentially lose it?
fractalEdited: has been edited. Do you want to save it before closing?
gifAvailable:
You have encoded gif available to save.
Do you want to save it?
gifAvailableCancel: Cancel will turn off gif encoding so you won't keep getting this warning again.
selectDestination: Select a destination to export to.
dontSave: Delete the available export and proceed to change this setting and reset the generation.
abortSave:
Delete the available export and proceed to change this setting and reset the generation.
Also turn the encodding OFF so this message is not spammed again.
pngsAvailable:
You have PNGs available to save or convert to Mp4.
Do you want to save it?
Cancel will turn off mp4 encoding so you won't keep getting this warning again.
pngsAvailableCancel: Cancel will turn off mp4 encoding so you won't keep getting this warning again.
cancelledText: Cancelled or empty name. Did not save the palette.
notForEndUser:
This mode is not really meant for the end user, it only generates all parameters and export a hash.txt file will all the unique ones.
If you actually want an animation of all seeds, AllSeeds is recommended instead as that doesn't waste resources doing the hashing and encodes the animation for export.
onlyPreview: This is only a low resolution preview image, please wait until the full resolution you have selected if finished.
notFinishedGenerating: Animation is not finished generating yet.
ffmpeg: ffmpeg.exe not found. Are you sure you have downloaded the full release with ffmpeg included?
gifDisabled: You do not have GIF encoding enabled, pick a Local of Global GIF, and try again after it's done encoding.
gifToMp4Unavailable:
This Export type is currently broken and will be fixed later, or removed.
Just use regular MP4 export that converts PNGs, it has better quality anyway.
gifStillSaving: Export is still saving, either wait until it's finished, or click this button before saving a GIF.
mp4stillSaving: Export is still saving, wait until it's finished.
alreadyFractal: There already is a loaded fractal of the same name, selecting it.
noAngles: Type a unique name for the new set of children angles to the box on the left.
uniqueAngles:
There already is a set of children angles of the same name.
Type a new unique name to the box on the left.
noColors: Type a unique name for the new set of children colors to the box on the left.
uniqueColors:
There already is a set of children colors of the same name.
Type a new unique name to the box on the left.
noCutFunctions: There are no CutFunctions left in the fractal.
bloomLarge: bloom too large for even a single ApplyDot to fit into cache
bloomLarge2: bloom too large for the tasks painting into stripes to not overlap the kernels
editSwitch: Switch these two points in the list. (Only has effects on cutFunctions, or if you switch the main first one.
editC: Color shift of the child in halves. (2 in RGB: RGB->GBR, 4 in RGB: RGB->BRG, 3 in RGB: RGB->CPY, 2 in YB: YB->BY)
